utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality hiring plan than wo_skills. The most significant differentiator is J2 (fully-loaded compensation budgeting): wi_skills explicitly applies a ~1.3x overhead multiplier to every role (e.g., $75K base → $104K fully-loaded), while wo_skills lists only raw base salaries with no overhead calculation — a critical operational gap. Both plans pass stage-appropriate role selection (J1), milestone-sequenced hiring (J3), FTE vs. contractor logic (J5), and budget feasibility (J6). Neither plan explicitly addresses recruiting lead time or ramp buffers (J4 fails for both). The wi_skills plan is more actionable because founders can immediately validate total cash outflow against their 14-month runway using the fully-loaded figures, whereas the wo_skills figures would understate true costs by 30-40% and could lead to runway miscalculation.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher-quality plan overall. Both runs pass all verifier checks and address most evaluation criteria. utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality output than wo_skills. Both runs correctly differentiate the founder equity split (J1), recommend appropriate vesting (J7), link hires to milestones (J4), acknowledge cash constraints (J5), and provide pre-seed-calibrated equity ranges (J6). However, wi_skills clearly outperforms on two critical items: J2 (option pool pre-funding dilution mechanics) — wi_skills explicitly explains that the pool is created pre-money diluting founders, while wo_skills simply states the pool size without explaining who bears the dilution; and J3 (contractor-first discipline) — wi_skills explicitly categorizes design and marketing as contractor roles within the 2-5 person pre-seed team guidance, while wo_skills recommends FTE hires without a contractor-first framework. These two differentiators — pre-funding dilution explanation and contractor-first hiring discipline — are precisely the pre-seed-specific insights the skill is designed to provide, making the wi_skills output materially more investor-ready and defensible.

The skill-equipped agent produced a more rigorous and board-ready plan: (1) it used structured Python calculations to validate compensation against ARR constraints with precision ($53,700 headroom vs ceiling); (2) it applied correct Series A option pool sizing (4.5% top-up vs wo_skills' egregious 12% top-up recommendation); (3) it explicitly documented executive search timelines of 16-24 weeks for VP hires; (4) department allocations were precisely benchmarked at 40% Engineering. The wo_skills agent produced a surprisingly strong output without the skill, likely because the board_requirements.md file already encoded many constraints, but it lacked the skill-derived benchmark grounding that would make the plan more defensible under board scrutiny. Overall: wi_skills is more effective.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, investor-ready pre-seed strategy documents that passed all 7 evaluation criteria. The wi_skills run invoked the team-composition-analysis skill via a subagent task, which returned structured recommendations that were then incorporated into a comprehensive document (48/37/15 split, detailed option pool dilution mechanics, contractor-first hiring, milestone-gated hires, below-market comp, pre-seed equity ranges, and 4-year/1-year cliff vesting). The wo_skills run relied on the agent's general knowledge and produced a similarly complete document (46/37/17 split) with the same structural elements. The wi_skills output is marginally more detailed — particularly in the option pool dilution illustration with an example cap table and the negotiation range guidance — but both documents are substantively equivalent in covering all required evaluation dimensions. The skill provided structured scaffolding that resulted in slightly more precise pre-seed calibration (e.g., more granular equity ranges, explicit prior-service vesting credit), making wi_skills marginally superior, but the wo_skills document is also of high quality and would pass investor scrutiny.
